notes: HARD RULE (LS): qore ≠ king. Never gloss as 'king/monarch/sovereign ruler'. If sovereign authority is encoded, the corpus uses mlo. When qore precedes later-king names, treat as pre-accession heir title or delegated/regional rulership, not kingship. FORBIDDEN: king/monarch/sovereign-ruler gloss by default; sovereignty requires mlo. Constraint: qore never equals mlo (king). Any translation or phase text that renders `qore` as 'king' is a mis-specification; correct to heir/prince/regional lord.
